While there was genetic structure among biogeographic provinces in *P. tapeinosoma*, the occurrence of a single haplotype across the entire range (except the East Pacific and Marquesas) indicates a highly dispersive life history with rare dispersals across barriers leading to divergence and/or speciation. With regard to morphology, *P. azaleus* had the lowest meristic counts, Indo-Pacific *P. tapeinosoma* the highest, and *P. goslinei* and Marquesan *P. tapeinosoma* intermediate. In combination with population genetic and ancestral range analyses, this presents the possibility of an ancient East Pacific origin, with rare dispersal to the Central Pacific giving rise to the Marquesan, Hawaiian, and Indo-Pacific clades/haplogroups. Due to the isolation of Hawai&#x02BB;i (by distance) and the Marquesas (primarily by the configuration of oceanographic currents), these groups underwent only slight morphological divergence from their ancestral population. In contrast, *P. tapeinosoma* dispersed throughout the rest of the Indo-Pacific and experienced significant increases in meristic character counts.

<!--, but the initial description of *P. goslinei* [@strasburgNotesBlennioidFishes1956] suggested that it was intermediate between *P. azaleus* and *P. tapeinosoma*. The placement of *P. azaleus* as the sister group to *P. tapeinosoma* and *P. goslinei* in this study was not supported by the `MrBayes` tree, as it nested within mixed groups of *P. tapeinosoma* and *P. goslinei*. However, it occurred on a long internal branch (and was supported in the `BEAST` tree). Thus, its placement was likely an artifact of our limited dataset. It is doubtless that these three species form an evolutionary unit. Ancestral range reconstructions suggest they may have originated in the Tropical East Pacific, where *P. azaleus* is endemic. Previous studies have shown evidence for dispersal in both directions across the East Pacific barrier [cite,cite]

and further study of these lineages could determine whether *P. azaleus* originally colonized the Eastern Pacific through Hawai&#x02BB;i or merely shares a common ancestor.  and contribute to the growing literature about whether isolated islands with high levels of endemism export biodiversity to other regions [@bowenOriginsTropicalMarine2013]. On the other hand, both the *P. azaleus* and Marquesan *P. tapeinosoma* haplotypes were linked to the rest of *P. tapeinosoma* through Indian Ocean haplotypes (rather than those from the Pacific which are much closer). This may indicate that the divergence of these lineages dates back to the closure of the Tethys Sea (that divided the tropical Atlantic and Indo-Pacific realms) as observed in many other reef fish groups [@floeterAtlanticReefFish2008].

### *rhinorhynchos* species group {-}

hello sailor

<!--The taxonomy and phylogeography of fangblennies was elucidated in this study by including more species than ever before in a molecular phylogeny of *Plagiotremus*. Although only one of the Hawaiian lineages seems to merit full species status, further study of the phenotypic and genetic variation within the "*tapeinosoma*" sub-group could lead to the description of distinct species in other regions, as well as provide further insight into how endemic marine species evolve in some of the world's most isolated archipelagos.-->

# Figures and Tables {-}

![Endemic Hawaiian *Plagiotremus* species. (a) `r spp$cite('Plagiotremus goslinei')`. (b) `r spp$cite('Plagiotremus ewaensis')`. Photo credit: Keoki Stender ([https://marinelifephotography.com/](https://marinelifephotography.com/)), with permission.](data/images/goslinei-ewaensis.png){#fig:fig_plagiotremus width=75%}

\newpage

```{r fig-map, fig.cap='Map of *Plagiotremus* collection localities across the Indo-Pacific. Background shading indicates biogeographic realm, yellow: Western Indo-Pacific, green: Central Indo-Pacific, red: Eastern Indo-Pacific, orange: Tropical East Pacific. (a) *Plagiotremus goslinei* and *P. tapeinosoma*. (b) *Plagiotremus ewaensis* and *P. rhinorhynchos*.', results="asis", fig.width=8, fig.height=10, dependson="init"}
# define our fake antemeridian
ante_meridian <- 165

# define a long & slim polygon that overlaps the meridian line & set its CRS to match
# that of world
offset <- 180 - ante_meridian
polygon <- st_polygon(x = list(rbind(
  c(-0.0001 - offset, 90),
  c(0 - offset, 90),
  c(0 - offset, -90),
  c(-0.0001 - offset, -90),
  c(-0.0001 - offset, 90)
))) %>%
  st_sfc() %>%
  st_set_crs(4326)


# ocean background
offset <- ante_meridian+180

# our target crs transformation. maybe we did this twice but I'll leave it since it works
target_crs <- st_crs(str_glue("+proj=moll +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +lat_0=0 +lon_0={ante_meridian}"))


# realms we want to show on the map
map_regions <- regions %>%
  filter(str_detect(REALM,fixed("pacific",ignore_case = TRUE)) & !str_detect(REALM,fixed("temperate",ignore_case = TRUE)))

# Convert to polygon geometries, correct geometry errors, project to PCS,
# snap vertices, merge polygons by REALM, project back to original CRS
# this gets rid of the vertical line at 180, at least in theory
map_regions <- suppressWarnings(
  map_regions %>%
    st_cast("POLYGON") %>%
    st_make_valid() %>%
    st_transform(target_crs) %>%
    st_snap(x = ., y = ., tolerance = 165) %>%
    summarise(geometry = st_union(geometry), .by = REALM) %>%
    st_transform(4326) %>%
    st_shift_longitude() %>%
    st_transform(target_crs)
)

# create world map without overlaps and weird artifacts
worldrn <- suppressWarnings(
  ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf") %>%
    st_make_valid() %>%
    st_difference(polygon) %>%
    st_transform(crs = target_crs)
)


# make the ocean background fill
jitter = 0.01
lat <- c(seq(from=-90,to=90,by=0.25))
lon <- c(rep(offset-jitter,length(lat)),rep(offset+jitter,length(lat)))

oceans <- cbind(lon, c(lat, rev(lat))) %>%
  st_linestring() %>%
  st_cast("POLYGON") %>%
  st_sfc(crs=4326) %>%
  st_shift_longitude() %>%
  st_transform(crs=paste0("+proj=moll +lon_0=",ante_meridian," +lat_0=0 +x_0=0 +y_0=0"))

# create point data
mapping_data <- all_samples %>%
  filter(species %in% unlist(species_pairs)) %>%
  filter(source == 'sequenced') %>%
  drop_na(lat,lon) %>%
  st_as_sf(coords=c('lon','lat'),remove=FALSE,crs=4326) %>%
  dplyr::rename(Species=species) %>%
  st_transform(target_crs) %>%
  st_jitter()

# get unique species
spp <- unique(mapping_data$Species)

# make shape scale
shapos <- c(21:24) %>%
  set_names(spp)
# make fill scale
fillers <- paletteer_d('wesanderson::Darjeeling1',length(spp)) %>%
  set_names(spp)

# make two world plots with the different species pairs
plotz <- species_pairs %>%
  purrr::map(\(pair) {
    # get only species in pair
    pd <- mapping_data %>%
      filter(Species %in% pair)

    # make the pllot
    ggplot() +
      geom_sf(data = oceans, fill="lightblue", alpha=0.3) +
      geom_sf(data = worldrn, aes(group = admin), fill="lightgrey") +
      geom_sf(data = map_regions, aes(fill = REALM),show.legend = FALSE, alpha = 0.3, color="grey77") +
      scale_fill_paletteer_d('waRhol::skull_76_1',guide="none") +
      new_scale_fill() +
      geom_sf(data = pd,  aes(fill=Species, shape=Species), size=2, color="black" ) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = fillers, guide = guide_legend(title=NULL)) +
      scale_shape_manual(values=shapos, guide = guide_legend(title=NULL)) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.key = element_blank(),
        legend.text = element_text(size=12,face='italic')
      )
  })

# reduce the plots with `/` so they're stacked on top of one another
plotz %>%
  purrr::reduce(`/`) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'a') &
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(face='bold'))
  # theme(plot.tag = element_text(face='bold',margin = margin(r=4,b=4)))
cat("{#fig:fig_map}")
```

\newpage

```{r trees, results="asis", dependson="alignment"}
# read complete COI tree and join sample data
tree_covg <- names(alignments$coi)

trees <- tree_covg %>%
  sort() %>%
  set_names() %>%
  purrr::map(\(covg) {
    tree <- read.mrbayes(here('data','trees',str_glue('coi_{covg}.nex.con.tre'))) %>%
      left_join(all_samples,by=c(label='id'))

    # drop all but one Omobranchus tip to simplify things
    to_drop <- tree %>%
      as_tibble() %>%
      filter(
        genus == 'Omobranchus',
        label != 'Oan-AMS266-08'
      ) %>%
      pull(label)

    tree <- tree %>%
      drop.tip(to_drop)

    # figure out our one omobranchus outgroup node
    outgroup <- tree %>%
      as_tibble() %>%
      filter(genus == 'Omobranchus') %>%
      pull(node)
    if (length(outgroup) > 1) {
      outgroup <- getMRCA(tree@phylo,outgroup)
    }

    # reroot the tree on the omobranchus node
    tree@phylo <- reroot(
      tree@phylo,
      outgroup,
      position = tree@phylo$edge.length[which(tree@phylo$edge[,2]==outgroup)]/2
    )

    # probs get set to NA when tree is rerooted
    # here we're doing essentially what figtree does
    tree <- tree %>%
      mutate(
        prob = case_when(
          !isTip & is.na(prob) ~ "1",
          .default = prob
        ),
        prob_cut = cut(as.numeric(prob),c(-Inf,0.9,0.99,Inf),labels=c('<0.9','≥0.9','≥0.99'))
      )

    # create groups to color the tree sections
    genus_groups <- tree %>%
      as_tibble() %>%
      filter(genus != 'Omobranchus') %>%
      split(.$genus) %>%
      imap(\(grp,genus) {
        unique(grp$label)
      })
    tree <- tree %>%
      mutate(lbl = case_when(
        genus == 'Omobranchus' ~ 'Omobranchus',
        .default = ''
      )) %>%
      groupOTU(genus_groups,'Genus')
    # make the groups be a factor
    attr(tree@phylo,'Genus') <- factor(attr(tree@phylo,'Genus'),levels=names(genus_groups))

    tree
  })
```

```{r fig-tree-genera, fig.cap='COI Bayesian consensus trees for *Plagiotremus* and closely-related genera. (a) Full tree based on 50% coverage alignment. (b) Genus-level tree based on full alignment (all sequences). (c) Genus-level tree based on 99% coverage alignment.', results="asis", fig.width=8, fig.height=5, dependson="trees"}
# full tree
ft <- 'c50'
# subtrees
st <- grep('c50',names(trees),invert = TRUE,value = TRUE)

# calculate MRCA of genera for clade labeling, excluding Omobranchus
genera <- trees %>%
  purrr::map(\(tree) {
    tree %>%
      as_tibble() %>%
      filter(genus != 'Omobranchus') %>%
      split(.$genus) %>%
      imap(\(grp,genus) {
        if (nrow(grp) > 1) {
          node <- getMRCA(tree@phylo,grp$node)
        } else {
          node <- grp$node
        }
        tibble(id=node,text=genus)
      }) %>%
      list_rbind()
  })

onodes <- trees %>%
  purrr::map(\(tree) {
    tree %>%
      as_tibble() %>%
      filter(genus == 'Omobranchus') %>%
      pull(node)
  })

# gcol <- c(paletteer_d('lisa::MaxErnst',3),'#000000')
# gcol <- c(paletteer_d('lisa::M_C_Escher',3),'#000000')
gcol <- c(set_names(paletteer_d('lisa::WassilyKandinsky_1',3),genera$c00$text),'0'='#000000')

gnodes <- genera[[ft]] %>%
  pull(id) %>%
  combn(2) %>%
  apply(MARGIN=2,FUN=\(nodes) {
    getMRCA(trees[[ft]]@phylo,nodes)
  }) %>%
  unique()

pointcol <- c('≥0.99' = 'black','≥0.9' = 'darkgrey')

# plot the tree
full_tree <- ggtree(trees %>% pluck(ft),aes(color=Genus),size=0.7) +
  geom_cladelab(mapping=aes(node=id,label=text,color=Genus),data=genera %>% pluck(ft), align=FALSE, fontface = "bold.italic", offset = 0.005)  +
  geom_cladelabel(onodes %>% pluck(ft),'bolditalic(Omobranchus)', offset = 0.005, parse=TRUE, size=3) +
  geom_point2(aes(fill=prob_cut,subset = node %in% gnodes),shape=21,size=2,show.legend = TRUE) +
  scale_y_reverse() +
  geom_rootedge(0.005) +
  scale_color_manual(values=gcol,guide='none') +
  scale_fill_manual(values=pointcol,drop=FALSE,name='Node support\n(posterior probability)') +
  theme(
    legend.position = 'inside',
    legend.justification = 'left',
    legend.position.inside = c(0,0.1),
    legend.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  xlim(0,0.55)


subtrees <- trees[st] %>%
  imap(\(tree,covg) {
    gg <- genera %>%
      pluck(covg)

    gnodes <- gg %>%
      pull(id) %>%
      combn(2) %>%
      apply(MARGIN=2,FUN=\(nodes) {
        getMRCA(tree@phylo,nodes)
      }) %>%
      unique()

    gg$id %>%
      purrr::reduce(\(g,node) g %>% collapse(node=node),.init=ggtree(tree,aes(color=Genus))) +
      geom_cladelab(mapping=aes(node=id,label=text,color=Genus),data=gg, align=FALSE, fontface = "bold.italic", offset = 0.001)  +
      geom_point2(aes(fill=prob_cut,subset = node %in% gnodes),shape=21,size=2) +
      geom_cladelabel(onodes %>% pluck(covg),'bolditalic(Omobranchus)', parse=TRUE) +
      geom_point2(aes(subset = (node %in% gg$id)),shape=18,size=3) +
      geom_rootedge(0.005) +
      xlim(NA,0.3) +
      scale_color_manual(values=gcol,guide='none')  +
      scale_fill_manual(values=pointcol,guide='none') +
      scale_y_reverse() +
      xlim(0,0.28)
  })

# plot layout
layout <- '
  11122
  11133
'
# build the plot
c(full_tree,subtrees) %>%
  purrr::reduce(`+`) +
  plot_layout(design=layout) &
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'a') &
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(face='bold'))

cat("{#fig:fig_tree_genera}")
```

\newpage

```{r fig-tree-plagiotremus, fig.cap='COI Bayesian consensus tree of relationships within *Plagiotremus* based on 50% coverage alignment. Dashed magenta edges indicate individuals collected in Hawai&#x02BB;i. Node labels indicate Bayesian posterior probability.', results="asis", fig.width=9, fig.height=7, dependson="trees"}
# drop non-Plagiotremus tips
to_drop <- trees$c50 %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  filter(genus != 'Plagiotremus') %>%
  pull(label)

# filter the tree
tree <- trees$c50 %>%
  drop.tip(to_drop)
# save the tip labels
tips <- tree@phylo$tip.label

hap <- haplotypes %>%
  pluck('pgt','coi')

# get haplotypes
# generate haplotype index map
hindex <- attr(hap$haplotypes,'index') %>%
  imap(\(idx,h) {
    tibble(
      haplotype = h,
      sequence = rownames(hap$alignment)[idx]
    )
  }) %>%
  list_rbind()

# join metadata to tree
# and try to define some geographic range info
tree <- tree %>%
  left_join(hindex,by=c(label='sequence')) %>%
  left_join(hap$samples,by=c(label='id'))

# get tips with marquesan haplotypes
marquesan_tips <- tree %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  group_by(haplotype) %>%
  filter(province == 'Marquesas') %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  pull(label)

# get root node and all children for marquesan haplotypes
marquesan_node <- getMRCA(tree@phylo,marquesan_tips)
marquesas <- ggtree:::getSubtree(tree@phylo,marquesan_node)

# make nice-looking clades we can label
clades <- tree %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  drop_na(label) %>%
  mutate(
    grp = case_when(
      str_detect(species,'rhinorhynchos') ~ 'P. rhinorhynchos',
      str_detect(species,'ewaensis') ~ 'P. ewaensis',
      str_detect(species,'ewaensis') ~ 'P. ewaensis',
      str_detect(species,'flavus|laudandus') ~ 'P. laudandus/flavus',
      str_detect(species,'townsendi') ~ 'P. townsendi',
      str_detect(species,'phenax') ~ 'P. phenax',
      str_detect(species,'azaleus') ~ 'P. azaleus',
      str_detect(species,'tapeinosoma|goslinei') ~ 'P. tapeinosoma/goslinei',
      .default = ''
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    grp = case_when(
      node %in% marquesas & !is.na(label) ~ 'P. cf. tapeinosoma\n(Marquesas)',
      .default = grp
    ),
  ) %>%
  split(.$grp) %>%
  imap(\(grp,clade) {
    if (nrow(grp) > 1) {
      node <- getMRCA(tree@phylo,grp$node)
    } else {
      node <- grp$node
    }
    tibble(id=node,text=clade)
  }) %>%
  list_rbind() %>%
  mutate(
    offset = case_when(
      str_detect(text,'azaleus') ~ -0.001,
      str_detect(text,'goslinei') ~ -0.08,
      .default = 0
    )
  )

# get the rode node, because we're gonna set its probability to 1
rootnode <- getMRCA(tree@phylo,tree@phylo$tip.label)

# update some of the internal data for the tree
tree <- tree %>%
  mutate(
    prob = case_when(
      node == rootnode ~ 1,
      .default = as.numeric(prob)
    ),
    posterior = cut(
      prob,
      c(-Inf,0.60,0.75,0.999,Inf),
      labels=c("<0.60","0.60-0.75","0.75-0.95","1")
    )
  )

# figure out which nodes to put points on
nodepoints <- clades %>%
  pull(id) %>%
  combn(2) %>%
  apply(MARGIN=2,FUN=\(nodes) {
    getMRCA(tree@phylo,nodes)
  }) %>%
  unique()

# make colors for our node points
clr_map <- c("<0.60"="white","0.60-0.75"="gray75","0.75-0.95"="gray40","1"="black")

tree <- groupOTU(tree, tree@phylo$tip.label[grep("Pew|Pgo", tree@phylo$tip.label, invert = TRUE)])

ggtree(tree,aes(color=group,linetype=group)) +
  scale_y_reverse() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c('#C82B84','black'),guide='none') +
  scale_linetype_manual(values=c(2,1), guide='none') +
  geom_cladelab(mapping=aes(node=id,label=text,offset=offset),data=clades, parse=TRUE,fontface='bold.italic', barsize=1) +
  geom_rootedge(0.005) +
  geom_nodelab(aes(subset=(node %in% nodepoints),label=round(prob,2)),hjust=0,geom='text',fontface='bold',size=3) +#nudge_x = -0.003, nudge_y=1.1) +
  theme(legend.position = c(0.1,0.25)) +
  xlim(NA,0.25) +
  geom_treescale(y=-135,x=0,offset=1)
cat("{#fig:fig_tree_plagiotremus}")
```

\newpage

```{r fig-hap-coi-pgt, fig.cap='COI haplotype network of *Plagiotremus tapeinosoma* and *P. goslinei*. Abbreviations: RS: Red Sea, WIO: Western Indian Ocean, CIP: Central Indo-Pacific, CP: Central Pacific, PL: Polynesian Province, MHI: Main Hawaiian Islands, NWHI: Northwestern Hawaiian Islands, MQ: Marquesas.', results="asis", fig.width=16, fig.height=10, dependson="haplotypes"}
haplotypes$pgt$coi$plot
cat("{#fig:fig_hap_coi_pgt}")
```

\newpage

```{r fig-hap-cytb-pgt, fig.cap='cytb haplotype network of *Plagiotremus tapeinosoma* and *P. goslinei*. Abbreviations as in [@fig:fig_hap_coi_pgt].', results="asis", fig.width=16, fig.height=10, dependson="haplotypes"}
haplotypes$pgt$cytb$plot
cat("{#fig:fig_hap_cytb_pgt}")
```

\newpage

```{r fig-hap-coi-per, fig.cap='COI haplotype network of *Plagiotremus rhinorhynchos* and *P. ewaensis*. Abbreviations as in [@fig:fig_hap_coi_pgt].', results='asis', fig.width=16, fig.height=10, dependson='haplotypes'}
haplotypes$per$coi$plot
cat("{#fig:fig_hap_coi_per}")
```

\newpage

```{r fig-tree-ancestral-range, fig.cap='Maximum clade credibility tree showing ancestral range reconstructions for the *tapeinosoma* species group. Node pies indicate likelihood of reconstructed origins. Annotated nodes show most likely origin and likelihood.', results='asis', fig.width=10, fig.height=8, dependson=c('beast-tree','haplotypes')}
# figure out our color palette
origins <- tibble(
  a = provinces,
  b = provinces
) %>%
  mutate(a = if_else(str_detect(a,'Hawaii'),'Hawaii',a)) %>%
  group_by(a) %>%
  mutate( color = paletteer_d( palette_map[as.character(cur_group()$a)],direction=-1)[1:n()], n = n() ) %>%
  ungroup()
beast_pal <- origins %>%
  select(b,color) %>%
  deframe()

# create ancestral range pie subplots and color them appropriately
pies <- nodepie(ancestral_states,cols=2:10)
pies <- lapply(pies,\(x) x + scale_fill_manual(values=beast_pal))

# update tree with pretty labels
beast_tree <- beast_tree %>%
  mutate(
    label = case_when(
      !is.na(label) ~ location,
      .default = label
    ),
    loc = map2_chr(location,location.prob,\(l,p) str_glue('{province_abbrev[as.character(l)]}\n({pc(round(p,3),0.1)})')),
    height = as.numeric(height)
  )

# get color for root edge (which might not work)
rc <- beast_tree %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  filter(node == rn) %>%
  pull(location)
rc <- beast_pal[rc]

# get legend labels
legend_labs <- beast_tree %>%
  pull(location) %>%
  levels()
legend_labs <- str_glue('{legend_labs} ({province_abbrev[legend_labs]})')

# plot tree in reverse timescale
plotz <- revts(ggtree(beast_tree,aes(color = location), key_glyph='rect',size=1)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=beast_pal, labels=legend_labs,name='Province') +
  geom_inset(pies,width = 0.05, height = 0.05) +
  geom_nodelab(
    aes(subset = (node %in% clade_nodes), label = loc),
    color='black',
    nudge_x = 0.07,
    hjust = 0
  )

# get subset of nodes for which to show pies
rm <- root_map %>%
  filter(id %in% c('pta','pgo','paz','pta_mq'))

# we have to do this because geom_cladelab doesn't support parsing plotmath for some reason
# label relevant clades
reduce2(
  rm$node,rm$group,
  \(p,x,y) p + geom_cladelabel(node=x,label=y,parse=TRUE,offset=0.1,offset.text = 0.1),
  .init = plotz
) +
  theme_tree2() +
  theme(
    legend.position = 'inside',
    legend.position.inside = c(0.15,0.85)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = pretty(c(-beast_tree@data$height,0),n = 5), guide = guide_axis(cap = 'both'),limits = c(-5.2,1), labels = abs) +
  xlab('Ma before present')

cat("{#fig:fig_tree_ancestral_range}")
```

\newpage

```{r tbl-collection,results="asis",dependson="init"}
tbl_collection <- original_samples %>%
  left_join(plagiotremus_data,by="id",suffix=c("","_sequenced")) %>%
  group_by(species) %>%
  summarise(
    Range = ifelse(cur_group()$species %in% hawaii_species,'Hawai&#x02BB;i','Indo-Pacific'),
    n = n(),
    `COI sequences` = sum(sequenced_coi),
    `cytb sequences` = sum(sequenced_cytb)
  ) %>%
  mutate(species = str_glue("*{species}*")) %>%
  dplyr::rename(Species = species)
print_table(tbl_collection,"tbl_collection","Specimen collection summary.",hdr_case=NA,accuracy=1)
```

\newpage

```{r tbl-stats-coi-pgt, results="asis", dependson="haplotypes"}
hap <- haplotypes$pgt$coi

hap_stats <- hap$samples %>%
  group_by(province) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    S = length(seg.sites(hap$alignment[id,])),
    H = nrow(pegas::haplotype(hap$alignment[id,])),
    h = hap.div(hap$alignment[id,]),
    `&#x03c0;` = nuc.div(hap$alignment[id,]),
    D = tajima.test(hap$alignment[id,])$D,
    pval = tajima.test(hap$alignment[id,])$Pval.beta,
    sig = case_when(
      pval < 0.001 ~ 3,
      pval < 0.01 ~ 2,
      pval < 0.05 ~ 1,
      .default = 0
    ),
    llon = mean(llon,na.rm=T)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate( sig = map_chr(sig,\(s) str_c(rep('\\*',s),collapse="")) ) %>%
  mutate(
    D = case_when(
      pval < 0.05 ~ str_glue('**{round(D,3)}^{sig}^**'),
      is.nan(D) ~ NA,
      .default = as.character(round(D,3))
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(llon) %>%
  select(-c(sig,pval,llon)) %>%
  dplyr::rename(Province=province)

print_table(hap_stats,id="tbl_stats_coi_pgt","COI population genetic summary statistics for *P. goslinei*/*tapeinosoma* across marine biogeographic provinces (n = number of samples, S = number of segregating sites, H = number of unique haplotypes, h = haplotype diversity, &#x03c0; = nucleotide diversity, D = Tajima's D; results in bold are statistically significant, \\*: *p* &lt; 0.05, \\*\\*: *p* &lt; 0.01, \\*\\*\\*: *p* &lt; 0.001).",accuracy=0.001,hdr_case = 'none',roundr=3,na='---')
```

\newpage

```{r amova-tbl, include=FALSE}
amova_tbl <- function(mova,roundr=3,adjust=FALSE, alpha=0.05, upper_p = FALSE) {
  phi <- mova$PhiST
  pval <- mova$p

  if (adjust != FALSE & adjust %in% p.adjust.methods) {
    pval[lower.tri(pval)] <- p.adjust(pval[lower.tri(pval)],adjust)
  }

  if (upper_p) {
    phi[upper.tri(phi)] <- t(pval)[upper.tri(t(pval))]
  }

  sig_idx <- !is.na(pval) & pval < alpha
  sig_idx <- sig_idx * 1
  sig_idx[pval < 0.01] <- 2
  sig_idx[pval < 0.001] <- 3

  phi <- round(phi,roundr)

  sig_n <- sig_idx[sig_idx > 0]
  sig_phi <- phi[sig_idx > 0]
  sig_rep <- map_chr(sig_n,\(n) str_c(rep("\\*",n),collapse=""))

  mode(phi) <- 'character'
  phi[sig_idx > 0] <- str_glue('**{sig_phi}^{sig_rep}^**')
  as.data.frame(phi)
}
```

```{r tbl-amova-pgt-coi, results="asis", dependson=c("amovas","amova-tbl")}
print_table(amova_tbl(amovas$pgt$coi$pairwise,adjust="BY",upper_p=TRUE),id="tbl_amova_pgt_coi","Pairwise COI &#x03A6;~st~ (below diagonal) and associated *p*-values (above diagonal) among biogeographic provinces (where n &gt; 5) for *Plagiotremus goslinei*/*P. tapeinosoma*. Significant comparisons appear in bold. Abbreviations: RS: Red Sea, WIO: Western Indian Ocean, CIP: Central Indo-Pacific, CP: Central Pacific, PL: Polynesian Province, MHI: Main Hawaiian Islands, NWHI: Northwestern Hawaiian Islands, MQ: Marquesas.",accuracy=0.001,hdr_case = 'none',roundr=3,na='---')
```

\newpage

```{r tbl-stats-cytb-pgt, results="asis", dependson="haplotypes"}
hap <- haplotypes$pgt$cytb

hap_stats <- hap$samples %>%
  group_by(province) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    S = length(seg.sites(hap$alignment[id,])),
    H = nrow(pegas::haplotype(hap$alignment[id,])),
    h = hap.div(hap$alignment[id,]),
    `&#x03c0;` = nuc.div(hap$alignment[id,]),
    D = tajima.test(hap$alignment[id,])$D,
    pval = tajima.test(hap$alignment[id,])$Pval.beta,
    sig = case_when(
      pval < 0.001 ~ 3,
      pval < 0.01 ~ 2,
      pval < 0.05 ~ 1,
      .default = 0
    ),
    llon = mean(llon,na.rm=T)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate( sig = map_chr(sig,\(s) str_c(rep('\\*',s),collapse="")) ) %>%
  mutate(
    D = case_when(
      pval < 0.05 ~ str_glue('**{round(D,3)}^{sig}^**'),
      is.nan(D) ~ NA,
      .default = as.character(round(D,3))
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(llon) %>%
  select(-c(sig,pval,llon)) %>%
  dplyr::rename(Province=province)

print_table(hap_stats,id="tbl_stats_cytb_pgt","cytb population genetic summary statistics for *P. goslinei*/*tapeinosoma* across marine biogeographic provinces. Column names as in [@tbl:tbl_stats_coi_pgt].",accuracy=0.001,hdr_case = 'none',roundr=3,na='---')
```

\newpage

```{r tbl-amova-pgt-cytb, results="asis", dependson=c("amovas","amova-tbl")}
print_table(amova_tbl(amovas$pgt$cytb$pairwise,adjust="BY",upper_p=TRUE),id="tbl_amova_pgt_cytb","Pairwise cytb &#x03A6;~st~ (below diagonal) and associated *p*-values (above diagonal) among biogeographic provinces (where n &gt; 5) for *Plagiotremus goslinei*/*P. tapeinosoma*. Significant comparisons appear in bold.",accuracy=0.001,hdr_case = 'none',roundr=3,na='---')
```

\newpage

```{r tbl-stats-coi-per, results="asis", dependson="haplotypes"}
hap <- haplotypes$per$coi

hap_stats <- hap$samples %>%
  group_by(province) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    S = length(seg.sites(hap$alignment[id,])),
    H = nrow(pegas::haplotype(hap$alignment[id,])),
    h = hap.div(hap$alignment[id,]),
    `&#x03c0;` = nuc.div(hap$alignment[id,]),
    D = tajima.test(hap$alignment[id,])$D,
    pval = tajima.test(hap$alignment[id,])$Pval.beta,
    sig = case_when(
      pval < 0.001 ~ 3,
      pval < 0.01 ~ 2,
      pval < 0.05 ~ 1,
      .default = 0
    ),
    llon = mean(llon,na.rm=T)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate( sig = map_chr(sig,\(s) str_c(rep('\\*',s),collapse="")) ) %>%
  mutate(
    D = case_when(
      pval < 0.05 ~ str_glue('**{round(D,3)}^{sig}^**'),
      is.nan(D) ~ NA,
      .default = as.character(round(D,3))
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(llon) %>%
  select(-c(sig,pval,llon)) %>%
  dplyr::rename(Province=province)

print_table(hap_stats,id="tbl_stats_coi_per","COI population genetic summary statistics for *P. ewaensis*/*rhinorhynchos* across marine biogeographic province. Column names as in [@tbl:tbl_stats_coi_pgt].",accuracy=0.001,hdr_case = 'none',roundr=3,na='---')
```

\newpage

```{r tbl-amova-prh-coi, results="asis", dependson=c("amovas","amova-tbl")}
print_table(amova_tbl(amovas$prh$coi$pairwise,adjust="BY",upper_p=TRUE),id="tbl_amova_prh_coi","Pairwise COI &#x03A6;~st~ (below diagonal) and associated *p*-values (above diagonal) among biogeographic provinces (where n &gt; 5) for *Plagiotremus rhinorhynchos*. Significant comparisons appear in bold.",accuracy=0.001,hdr_case = 'none',roundr=3,na='---')
```

\newpage

# References {-}

<!-- show main bibliography -->
<div class="multi-refs"></div>

\newpage

# Supplemental material {label="S"}

```{r fig-supp-covg, fig.cap='COI alignment coverage by dataset filtering level.', results="asis", fig.width=6, fig.height=7, results="asis", dependson="alignment"}
covg_data <- alignments %>%
  pluck('coi') %>%
  imap(\(alignment,ds) {
    covg <- apply(alignment,2,\(bases) sum(bases != 4) / length(bases))
    tibble(pos = seq_along(covg), coverage = covg, dataset = ds)
  }) %>%
  list_rbind() %>%
  mutate(dataset = fct_reorder(dataset,-parse_number(dataset)))

verts <- covg_data %>%
  group_by(dataset) %>%
  summarise(min = min(pos), max = max(pos), first_cov=dplyr::first(coverage), last_cov=dplyr::last(coverage))
ggplot(covg_data) +
  geom_area(aes(x = pos, y = coverage, fill=dataset),alpha=0.7, stat="identity",position="identity") +
  geom_line(aes(x = pos, y = coverage, color = dataset)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x=min,xend=min,y=0,yend=first_cov,color=dataset),data=verts) +
  geom_segment(aes(x=max,xend=max,y=0,yend=last_cov,color=dataset),data=verts)  +
  facet_wrap(~dataset,ncol=1) +
  labs(x='Nucleotide position',y='Coverage (%)') +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_line(color="#f0f0f0")
  ) +
  guides(color='none',fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(alpha=1))) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_fill_paletteer_d('tvthemes::Pearl',direction = -1,labels=c('99%','50%','Full dataset'),name='Filtering') +
  scale_color_paletteer_d('tvthemes::Pearl',direction = -1,labels=c('99%','50%','Full dataset'))
cat("{#fig:fig_supp_covg}")
```

\newpage

```{r fig-supp-origin-prob, fig.cap='Likelihood of ancestral geographic origin by *tapeinosoma* species-group clade.', results='asis', fig.width=9, fig.height=7, dependson='beast-tree'}
ggplot(root_table %>%
  pivot_longer(-c(group,id),names_to = 'origin',values_to = 'prob') %>%
  mutate(origin = factor(origin,levels=provinces))) +
  geom_col(aes(x=group,y=prob,fill=origin),position='dodge') +
  scale_fill_manual(values=beast_pal,name='Origin') +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45,hjust=1)) +
  labs(x='Clade',y='Likelihood') +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = \(x) parse(text=x)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent)
cat("{#fig:fig_supp_origin_prob}")
```

\newpage

```{r fig-supp-meristics, fig.cap='Frequency distributions of select meristic characters in the *tapeinosoma* species group. (a) Anal-fin ray count. (b) Total dorsal-fin elements. (c) Total vertebrae.', results="asis", dependson="meristics", fig.height=4, fig.width=8}
spp_map <- c(
  'azaleus' = str_glue('italic(P.~azaleus)~(n=={mcounts$azaleus})'),
  'goslinei' = str_glue('italic(P.~goslinei)~(n=={mcounts$goslinei})'),
  'tapeinosoma' = str_glue('italic(P.~tapeinosoma)~(Indo-Pacific)~(n=={mcounts$tapeinosoma})'),
  'tapeinosoma_mq' = str_glue('italic(P.~tapeinosoma)~(Marquesas)~(n=={mcounts$tapeinosoma_mq})')
)

meristics %>%
  mutate(species = spp_map[species]) %>%
  split(.$measurement) %>%
  purrr::imap(\(vv,mm) {
    x <- str_replace(csq(x2[[mm]]),', ','<br>')
    ggplot(vv) +
      geom_density(aes(x=size,color=species),adjust=2,key_glyph='rect',linewidth = 1) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank()) +
      annotation_custom(gridtext::richtext_grob(x,0.7,0.6,gp = gpar(fontsize=8),hjust = 0)) +
      guides(color = guide_legend(nrow=2,byrow=TRUE))
  }) %>%
  purrr::reduce(`+`) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'a') +
  plot_layout(guides = 'collect',axis_titles = "collect") &
  labs(x='Count',y='Frequency') &
  # scale_color_paletteer_d('ltc::fernande', name='Species',labels=scales::label_parse())
  scale_color_paletteer_d('wesanderson::Darjeeling1', name='Species',labels=scales::label_parse(),direction = -1) &
  theme(legend.position = 'bottom')
cat("{#fig:fig_supp_meristics}")
```

\newpage

```{r fig-supp-meristic-boxes, fig.cap='Box plots of meristic values by species. (a) Anal-fin ray count. (b) Total dorsal-fin elements. (c) Total vertebrae.', results="asis", dependson="meristics", fig.height=4, fig.width=8}
spp_map <- c(
  'azaleus' = str_glue('italic(P.~azaleus)'),
  'goslinei' = str_glue('italic(P.~goslinei)'),
  'tapeinosoma' = str_glue('italic(P.~tapeinosoma)~(Indo~Pacific)'),
  'tapeinosoma_mq' = str_glue('italic(P.~tapeinosoma)~(Marquesas)')
)

meristics %>%
  mutate(species = spp_map[species]) %>%
  split(.$measurement) %>%
  purrr::map(\(mm) {
    plotz <- ggplot(mm) +
      geom_boxplot(aes(species,size,fill=species)) +
      labs(x='',y='Count') +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(
        # axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
      ) +
      guides(fill = guide_legend(nlabels=scales::label_parse(),col=2))
    # get categories without visible boxes
    pp <- ggplot_build(plotz)@data[[1]] %>%
      filter(ymin == ymax)
    if (nrow(pp) > 0) {
      plotz <- plotz +
        geom_segment(data=pp,aes(x=xmin,xend=xmax,y=middle,yend=middle, color=fill), size=1) +
        guides(color = guide_none())
    }
    plotz
  }) %>%
  purrr::reduce(`+`) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'a') +
  plot_layout(guides = 'collect',axis_titles = "collect") &
  scale_fill_paletteer_d('wesanderson::Darjeeling1', name='Species',labels=scales::label_parse(),direction = -1) &
  theme(legend.position = 'bottom')
cat("{#fig:fig_supp_meristic_boxes}")
```

\newpage

```{r tbl-supp-seqs,results="asis",dependson="haplotypes"}
seq_tbl <- plagiotremus_data %>%
  filter(source == 'sequenced') %>%
  mutate(species = str_glue('*{species}*')) %>%
  arrange(species,id) %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      contains('accession'),
      ~(\(a) str_glue("[{a}](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/nuccore/{a})",.na=''))(.x)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    `Sequence ID`=id,species,`Province ({Kulbicki})` = kulbicki_province,ecoregion,country,
    locality,`Accession ({COI})`=coi_accession,`Accession (cytb)`=cytb_accession
  )
# seq_tbl <- plagiotremus_data %>%
#   filter(source == 'sequenced') %>%
#   mutate(accession = "") %>%
#   group_by(species,kulbicki_province,ecoregion,country,locality) %>%
#   summarise(accession="",n = n()) %>%
#   ungroup() %>%
#   mutate(species = str_glue('***{species}***')) %>%
#   group_by(species) %>%
#   mutate(across(kulbicki_province:country,~if_else(duplicated(.x),"",.x))) %>%
#   ungroup() %>%
#   mutate(species = if_else(duplicated(species),"",species)) %>%
#   dplyr::rename(`Province ({Kulbicki})` = kulbicki_province, `{GenBank} Accession` = accession)

print_table(seq_tbl,"tbl_supp_seqs","Detailed locality metadata for specimens collected in this study.",hdr_case = 'sentence')
```

\newpage

```{r tbl-supp-bold-seqs,results="asis",dependson="bold-seqs"}
bold_seq_tbl <- bold_seqs$coi$samples %>%
  select(species,processid,bin_uri,genbank_accession,country,catalognum) %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    Species=species,
    `Process ID`=processid,
    `BIN`=bin_uri,
    `GenBank Accession`=genbank_accession,
    Country=country,
    `Catalog Number`=catalognum
  ) %>%
  arrange(Species)
print_table(bold_seq_tbl,'tbl_supp_bold_seqs','Summary of external sequences used in this study.',hdr_case=NA)
```

\newpage

```{r tbl-supp-alignment-models, results="asis", dependson="init"}
print_table(here('data','tables','models.csv'),'tbl_supp_models','Summary of best-fit paritioning models by COI alignment coverage')
```

\newpage

```{r tbl-supp-dist-pgt-coi, results="asis", dependson="dna-distances"}
print_table(dna_distances$pgt$coi$tbl,id="tbl_supp_dist_pgt_coi","Mean COI Kimura 2-parameter (K2P) distances among biogeographic provinces for *Plagiotremus goslinei*/*P. tapeinosoma*. Abbreviations: RS: Red Sea, WIO: Western Indian Ocean, CIP: Central Indo-Pacific, CP: Central Pacific, PL: Polynesian Province, MHI: Main Hawaiian Islands, NWHI: Northwestern Hawaiian Islands, MQ: Marquesas.",accuracy=0.001,hdr_case = 'none',roundr=3,na='---')
```

\newpage

```{r tbl-supp-dist-pgt-cytb, results="asis", dependson="dna-distances"}
print_table(dna_distances$pgt$cytb$tbl,id="tbl_supp_dist_pgt_cytb","Mean cytb Kimura 2-parameter (K2P) distances among biogeographic provinces for *Plagiotremus goslinei*/*P. tapeinosoma*.",accuracy=0.001,hdr_case = 'none',roundr=3,na='---')
```

\newpage

```{r tbl-supp-dist-per-coi, results="asis", dependson="dna-distances"}
print_table(dna_distances$per$coi$tbl,id="tbl_supp_dist_per_coi","Mean COI Kimura 2-parameter (K2P) distances among biogeographic provinces for *Plagiotremus ewaensis*/*P. rhinorhynchos*.",accuracy=0.001,hdr_case = 'none',roundr=3,na='---')
```
